description:
Goal
Define one compiler2-native fixture contract model for fixtures2 that can express roots, semantic edge assertions, metric assertions, and optional canonical snapshot assertions without reusing the old fixture_matrix dump-golden shape.

Signal
- fixtures2 has a documented contract surface and parsing model
- the contract chooses structured assertions first and snapshots only where dense pinning is justified
- docs clearly explain when to use metrics, semantic edges, or snapshots

Strategy
1. Decide the source-of-truth format for compiler2 fixture contracts (inline file header, sidecar, or minimal paired files) with single-file ergonomics as the default.
2. Define the assertion vocabulary: roots, metrics, semantic edges, snapshot opt-in, blessing behavior, and failure artifact paths.
3. Update fixture docs so compiler2 fixtures stop being an ad hoc corpus and become a first-class contract surface beside fixtures/.

Validation
- the parser/data model compiles and round-trips representative fixture metadata
- docs are concrete enough that later harness tickets can implement them without inventing semantics

Removal targets
- undocumented fixtures2 test conventions
- ambiguity about when exact snapshots are allowed versus when structured assertions are required
title: Define fixtures2 compiler contract model and docs
